title: FilesCtlProtocol
description: files-ctl wire protocol: request/response envelopes and payloads for every command the web UI may invoke on the desktop client. Transferred as JSON over the 'files-ctl' WebRTC data channel. Phase 9 introduced request/response; Phase 11 adds server-pushed event envelopes (status:'event') and the six transfer-control commands (files.upload.begin/complete, files.download.begin/complete, files.transfer.cancel, files.transfer.error).
description:root: Index object that references every named type in this schema so codegen (quicktype) walks into each $def and emits a class/interface for it. This object is NEVER serialized on the wire -- consumers use the individual types (FilesRequestEnvelope, FilesSuccessEnvelope, FilesErrorEnvelope, and the per-command request/response pairs).

FilesErrorCode.title: FilesErrorCode
FilesErrorCode.description: Stable machine-readable error codes. Codes are frozen for the lifetime of the protocol; add new codes rather than repurposing old ones. Human-readable messages are produced from these codes by the frontend i18n layer in Phase 12. Phase-11 additions (TRANSFER_NOT_FOUND, CANCELLED, STALLED, DISK_FULL) cover transfer-pipeline cancel races, stall pushes, and disk-full reports. Phase-12 additions (PERMISSION_READ, PERMISSION_WRITE, SOURCE_GONE, DESTINATION_GONE, NAME_CONFLICT) split permission errors by direction and add explicit codes for missing source/destination and destination-name collisions so the frontend can render actionable dialogs without parsing free-text messages.

InvalidNameReason.title: InvalidNameReason
InvalidNameReason.description: Refinement of INVALID_NAME errors so the UI can render a specific reason without parsing free-text messages.

FilesError.title: FilesError
FilesError.description: Structured error object carried in FilesErrorEnvelope.error. Code is stable; message is a fallback string the backend may supply for developer logs (the frontend should key off code for user-facing text). Data is a free-form object whose shape depends on code (e.g., INVALID_NAME uses { reason: InvalidNameReason }).

code.description: Stable machine-readable identifier for the error class.

message.description: Fallback human-readable description for logs / debugging. Not a user-facing string.

data.description: Optional structured context whose shape depends on code. For INVALID_NAME: { reason: InvalidNameReason }. For ALLOWLIST_VIOLATION: { path: string }. For IO_ERROR: { errno?: string }.

FileEntry.title: FileEntry
FileEntry.description: Metadata for a single file or directory within an allowlisted root.

name.description: Base name of the entry (no path separators).

path.description: Canonical absolute path of the entry as observed by the desktop after allowlist resolution.

isDirectory.description: True if the entry is a directory, false for regular files. Symlinks that escape the allowlist are never returned.

sizeBytes.description: Size in bytes. For directories, value is 0 and callers should ignore it.

modifiedUtc.description: Last-modified timestamp in ISO-8601 / RFC-3339 UTC form (e.g. 2026-04-24T08:25:31Z).

isHidden.description: True if the entry is hidden per the host platform: FileAttributes.Hidden on Windows, leading dot on POSIX. Frontend filters these out by default and exposes a 'Show hidden files' toggle.

FilesRequestEnvelope.title: FilesRequestEnvelope
FilesRequestEnvelope.description: Every request sent from the frontend to the desktop on the files-ctl channel. id is a UUID chosen by the caller; the same id appears on the matching response envelope.

payload.description: Command-specific request payload. Shape depends on command.

FilesSuccessEnvelope.title: FilesSuccessEnvelope
FilesSuccessEnvelope.description: Positive response envelope. result shape depends on the request command.

id.description: Same correlation id as the request.

status.description: Discriminator: always the literal string 'success' on this envelope.

result.description: Command-specific success result. May be null for commands whose response carries no data (e.g. files.delete).

FilesErrorEnvelope.title: FilesErrorEnvelope
FilesErrorEnvelope.description: Negative response envelope. status is always 'error' and error carries the structured FilesError.

id.description: Same correlation id as the request.

status.description: Discriminator: always the literal string 'error' on this envelope.

error.description: Structured error describing why the request failed.

FilesListRootsRequest.title: FilesListRootsRequest
FilesListRootsRequest.description: Request payload for files.listRoots. No parameters.

FilesListRootsResponse.title: FilesListRootsResponse
FilesListRootsResponse.description: Response payload for files.listRoots. Returns the allowlisted roots the caller is permitted to browse.

roots.description: Allowlisted roots exposed to the browser. Each entry is presented as a FileEntry with isDirectory=true.

FilesListRequest.title: FilesListRequest
FilesListRequest.description: Request payload for files.list. Lists immediate children of path (non-recursive).

path.description: Absolute canonical path to list. Must resolve inside an allowlisted root after symlink resolution.

FilesListResponse.title: FilesListResponse
FilesListResponse.description: Response payload for files.list. Mirrors the requested path and carries the enumerated entries.

path.description: Canonical path that was listed. Echoes the request after canonicalization.

entries.description: Direct children of path. Does not include '.' or '..'. Sort order is unspecified; the UI sorts client-side.

FilesMkdirRequest.title: FilesMkdirRequest
FilesMkdirRequest.description: Request payload for files.mkdir. Creates a single new subdirectory inside parentPath.

parentPath.description: Absolute canonical path of the parent directory. Must resolve inside an allowlisted root.

name.description: Name of the new directory. Must pass name validation (no reserved names, no illegal chars).

FilesMkdirResponse.title: FilesMkdirResponse
FilesMkdirResponse.description: Response payload for files.mkdir. Returns the path of the newly created directory.

path.description: Canonical path of the created directory.

FilesRenameRequest.title: FilesRenameRequest
FilesRenameRequest.description: Request payload for files.rename. Renames path to a sibling with newName; does not move across directories.

path.description: Absolute canonical path of the entry to rename.

newName.description: New base name (no path separators). Must pass name validation.

FilesRenameResponse.title: FilesRenameResponse
FilesRenameResponse.description: Response payload for files.rename. Returns the new canonical path.

path.description: Canonical path of the entry after rename.

FilesDeleteRequest.title: FilesDeleteRequest
FilesDeleteRequest.description: Request payload for files.delete. Deletes a file or empty directory; recursive deletion is explicitly out of scope for Phase 9.

FilesDeleteResponse.title: FilesDeleteResponse
FilesDeleteResponse.description: Response payload for files.delete. No fields; an empty object indicates success.

FilesMoveRequest.title: FilesMoveRequest
FilesMoveRequest.description: Request payload for files.move. Moves src to dst; both must resolve inside allowlisted roots.

dst.description: Absolute canonical destination path. Parent must exist.

mode.description: Conflict behavior when destination name already exists.

FilesMoveResponse.title: FilesMoveResponse
FilesMoveResponse.description: Response payload for files.move. Echoes the resolved src and dst.

src.description: Canonical source path as it was at request time.

dst.description: Canonical destination path after the move.

FilesCopyRequest.title: FilesCopyRequest
FilesCopyRequest.description: Request payload for files.copy. Copies a single file from src to dst. Directory copy is out of scope for Phase 9.

src.description: Absolute canonical source path (file, not directory).

dst.description: Absolute canonical destination path. Parent must exist.

mode.description: Conflict behavior when destination name already exists.

FilesCopyResponse.title: FilesCopyResponse
FilesCopyResponse.description: Response payload for files.copy. Echoes the resolved src and dst.

dst.description: Canonical destination path of the created copy.

FilesEventEnvelope.title: FilesEventEnvelope
FilesEventEnvelope.description: Server-pushed event envelope. Distinguished from request/response envelopes by status:'event'. Used for files.download.complete and files.transfer.error in Phase 11. The frontend's FilesChannelClient dispatches these to listeners registered by command name (NOT correlated by id).

payload.description: Event-specific payload. Shape depends on command.

FilesUploadBeginRequest.title: FilesUploadBeginRequest
FilesUploadBeginRequest.description: Request payload for files.upload.begin. Reserves a transferId on the desktop side for an upload (browser->desktop) and returns the .partial path that will receive bytes over the files-data channel.

parentPath.description: Absolute canonical path of the parent directory. Must resolve inside an allowlisted root.

name.description: Final base name of the uploaded file (no path separators). Must pass name validation.

size.description: Total size of the file in bytes as known to the browser. Used by the desktop to decide on disk-space and to verify completion.

mode.description: Conflict behavior when destination name already exists.

FilesUploadBeginResponse.title: FilesUploadBeginResponse
FilesUploadBeginResponse.description: Response payload for files.upload.begin. Returns the desktop-allocated transferId and the .partial path the desktop will write incoming chunks to.

transferId.description: Unsigned 32-bit identifier allocated by the desktop. The browser MUST echo this in every chunk header on files-data and in any subsequent files.upload.complete / files.transfer.cancel for this transfer.

partialPath.description: Canonical absolute path of the .partial file the desktop will write into. Renamed to the final name on files.upload.complete.

FilesUploadCompleteRequest.title: FilesUploadCompleteRequest
FilesUploadCompleteRequest.description: Request payload for files.upload.complete. Signals that all bytes have been sent on files-data. The desktop verifies the .partial size matches expectedBytes, then renames .partial to the final name.

transferId.description: Identifier returned by the matching files.upload.begin response.

expectedBytes.description: Total bytes the browser sent on files-data for this transfer. Desktop rejects if .partial size differs.

FilesUploadCompleteResponse.title: FilesUploadCompleteResponse
FilesUploadCompleteResponse.description: Response payload for files.upload.complete. Returns the canonical path of the final file after the .partial -> final rename.

path.description: Canonical absolute path of the finalized file.

FilesDownloadBeginRequest.title: FilesDownloadBeginRequest
FilesDownloadBeginRequest.description: Request payload for files.download.begin. Asks the desktop to start streaming the bytes of path over files-data. The desktop allocates a transferId and begins sending chunks.

path.description: Absolute canonical path of the file to download. Must resolve inside an allowlisted root and must be a regular file.

FilesDownloadBeginResponse.title: FilesDownloadBeginResponse
FilesDownloadBeginResponse.description: Response payload for files.download.begin. Returns the desktop-allocated transferId, total size, and base name so the browser can preallocate buffers and surface filename in UI.

transferId.description: Unsigned 32-bit identifier allocated by the desktop. Every files-data chunk header will carry this id.

size.description: Total size of the file in bytes as observed by the desktop at request time.

name.description: Base name of the file (no path separators). Used by the browser to suggest a Save-As filename.

FilesTransferCancelRequest.title: FilesTransferCancelRequest
FilesTransferCancelRequest.description: Request payload for files.transfer.cancel. Aborts an in-flight upload or download identified by transferId. Idempotent: cancelling an already-finished or already-cancelled transfer returns success.

transferId.description: Identifier of the transfer to cancel, as returned by files.upload.begin or files.download.begin.

reason.description: Why the cancel is being issued. Pinned at schema level to prevent drift; receivers MAY treat unknown values as 'user'.

FilesTransferCancelResponse.title: FilesTransferCancelResponse
FilesTransferCancelResponse.description: Response payload for files.transfer.cancel. No fields; an empty object indicates success.

FilesDownloadCompletePayload.title: FilesDownloadCompletePayload
FilesDownloadCompletePayload.description: Payload for the files.download.complete server-pushed event. Sent by the desktop on the files-ctl channel after the last chunk for transferId has been written to files-data, so the browser knows when to finalize / save the assembled bytes.

transferId.description: Identifier of the download that just finished.

totalBytes.description: Total bytes streamed. Browser uses this to verify reassembly length.

FilesTransferErrorPayload.title: FilesTransferErrorPayload
FilesTransferErrorPayload.description: Payload for the files.transfer.error server-pushed event. Sent by the desktop when an in-flight transfer fails (stall, IO error, disk full, etc). The frontend dispatches by command name and matches transferId to the active transfer.

transferId.description: Identifier of the transfer that failed.

error.description: Structured FilesError describing the failure (typical codes: STALLED, IO_ERROR, DISK_FULL, INTERNAL_ERROR).
